data_source_summary: Official Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; National Portal (Agriculture > Water Management > Watershed Development); PMKSY-WDC Guidelines (GoI)

carried_from_t4a.C2_rho_or_VI: PASS (estimated — ρ << 1.0 strongly indicated; exact ratio unverified due to absent extraction volume data)

Q35.r: The scheme targets 42 blocks in semi-arid/dry regions of Chhattisgarh (Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008; Economic Survey 2024-25: '42% of state area in monsoon-dependent regions'). Semi-arid/rainfed zones are the primary category under NDMA drought-prone block classification. The scheme's entire rationale — water conservation structures, drought-resistant crops, soil moisture conservation — is premised on documented recurrent drought stress. CGWB assessments of Chhattisgarh semi-arid blocks show groundwater stress in several districts. The scheme design is a direct policy response to climate-induced agricultural vulnerability. Q35 = Yes → T4.3 threshold upgrades from ≥ 1 to ≥ 2 (semi-annual monitoring required for drought-stressed commons). Note: specific NDMA block-level notification or CGWB over-exploited/critical block list not directly cited in programs.md; climate-stressed classification is inferred from semi-arid/drought-rainfed targeting rationale.
Q35.src: Official Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008 (semi-arid/dry target zone); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; NDMA drought-prone block framework (national reference)

Q34.r: Threshold applies: Q35 = Yes (climate-stressed) → T4.3 pass threshold upgraded to ≥ 2 (semi-annual or more frequent monitoring with community-accessible disclosure). Monitoring regime assessed at score 2 based on: (1) National Portal provides a public-accessible performance dashboard tracking groundwater level trends, water structure status (4,800 structures mapped), block-wise rainfall data, and beneficiary statistics — accessible to any user including community members. Groundwater level trends are sourced from CGWB piezometric monitoring, which operates on quarterly to bi-annual cycles in most districts. (2) CAG annual audit (FY 2024-25) provides annual government-level resource health assessment with sustainability outcomes reported. (3) Outcome Budget 2025-26 discloses annual performance metrics publicly. Combined: annual government audit (CAG) + continuous/quarterly portal dashboard with community-accessible groundwater and structure data = score 2. Note: Score 3 (real-time continuous dashboard) is not confirmed — portal update frequency not specified.
Q34.src: National Portal (scheme dashboard — groundwater trends, water structure map, performance data); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; CGWB monitoring (quarterly groundwater data source)

Q36.r: No explicit penalty or sanction mechanism for violation of replenishment/maintenance obligations is stated in Official Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008 or cited in CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25, Outcome Budget 2025-26, or National Portal documentation. The PMKSY cross-reference on the National Portal implies PMKSY-WDC fund-withholding provisions may apply, but this is not directly confirmed from the scheme-specific notification text available. CAG audit findings are accountability mechanisms but not penalties as defined by the framework (fund withholding, disqualification, reduced allocation). T4.4 = 0 for this scheme as documented.
Q36.src: Official Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; National Portal

Q37.r: Post-scheme continuity mechanism exists through the institutionally mandated Watershed Committees at village level established under Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008. These committees are constituted as permanent village-level governance bodies (not project-period-only bodies) for management of the 4,800 water conservation structures. Evidence of active post-scheme continuity: (1) CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 confirms 88% of structures remain functional, implying active committee-led O&M beyond initial construction; (2) Annual watershed management budget (₹20 crores) is a recurring scheme allocation, not a one-time investment, confirming institutional continuity financing; (3) Groundwater improvement in 92% of blocks (CAG) demonstrates ongoing commons stewardship. Under PMKSY-WDC (cross-referenced), completed watershed assets are formally vested in the Watershed Committee/GP for perpetual management. T4.5 = 1 on this basis.
Q37.src: Official Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008 (watershed committee mandate); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (88% structures functional; 92% blocks improved); Outcome Budget 2025-26; PMKSY-WDC Guidelines (GoI) — asset vesting provision

Q38.r: Community-led monitoring is not formally mandated in the scheme design. The National Portal performance dashboard and groundwater trend monitoring are operated by government agencies (state Agriculture/Water Resources department, CGWB). CAG audit is a government accountability mechanism. Watershed committees participate in implementation but no formal community monitoring role (social audit, participatory resource assessment, community-managed DWLR, Village Ecological Register) is documented in Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008 or other available sources. Monitoring is primarily departmental with community-facing (but not community-operated) disclosure through the portal. T4.6 = 0 — flagged as primary structural upgrade lever.
Q38.src: Official Notification No. 2008/CGN-26/2008; National Portal; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

pass_verification.C2_rho_or_VI: PASS (from T4a — estimated; ρ << 1.0 indicated by 92% block groundwater improvement; exact ratio unverified)
pass_verification.C3_T4.3: PASS (Q34 = 2; Q35 = Yes upgrades threshold to ≥ 2; portal dashboard + CAG annual audit meets semi-annual community-accessible disclosure requirement)
pass_verification.C4_T4.4_or_T4.5: PASS (Q36 = No, Q37 = Yes; Q37 satisfies the OR condition — watershed committees provide post-scheme continuity mechanism per PMKSY vesting and 88% functional structures evidence)
pass_verification.enh_T4.6_flag: GOVERNANCE GAP — T4.6 = No. Monitoring is exclusively departmental (portal, CAG, outcome budget); no mandated community-led monitoring role documented. Per SOC framework, T4.6 = 0 is the single most important upgrade lever for sustainability lock. Recommended action: mandate social audit provision or watershed committee-level participatory resource assessment in revised scheme guidelines. Note: combined with T2.4 = No, T2.5 = No, and T3.5 = No, this creates 4 absent enhancement variables — scheme will be classified 'SOC with Governance Gaps' if all 4 tests pass.
pass_verification.overall_T4: PASS (provisional — C2_rho_or_VI is estimated; overall classification dependent on verification of exact ρ ratio once irrigation extraction data is available)
